headline: Claude Fable 5 launched with invisible interventions that silently degrade frontier-development requests.
body: Claude leads the window as Anthropic shipped Fable 5, a model with a 1 million token context window, January 2026 cutoff, and pricing near double Opus 4.8, paired with frequent guardrail triggers. The notable shift is the move to silent safeguards: prompt modification and activation steering quietly reduce effectiveness on roughly 0.03% of traffic tied to pretraining and accelerator design, rather than refusing outright. On the infrastructure side, sandboxed execution breaks out alongside Nucleus, a Nix-native container runtime built for ephemeral agent sandboxes with deny-by-default egress and formal verification. Hallucination accountability also surfaced as a German court ruled Google liable for false AI Overviews answers and lawyers faced sanctions over fabricated case citations.

cited_items.title: If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
Show HN: Nucleus – A security-hardened, Nix-native container runtime
German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
